shared_properties: The results panel follows the package palette, gold trim language, and transparent-background UI compositing rules.
All text-safe zones are deterministic so runtime strings can be bound without manual layout nudging.
The modal assumes a fixed 1920x1080 target and centered presentation above gameplay.
differences: This asset is a large post-floor modal rather than a persistent gameplay HUD panel.
The atlas includes rank-accent variants so celebratory color emphasis can change without altering the text layout.
Decorative corners are more pronounced than the HUD because the panel appears only after gameplay control is suspended.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the shadow survival loop, mirror and prism puzzle rules, scale targets, HUD placements, and Bevy runtime constraints for Umbra Scale.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the twilight art deco tower mood, silhouette contrast, gold trims, and hard-edge lighting style.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load floor_clear_rank_panel.png during Loading and register atlas slices for neutral panel, S-rank accent, A-rank accent, and shard-summary variant states.
runtime_notes: Render the panel above gameplay and pause menus as a centered modal after the exit door transition finishes.
Leave safe zones for FLOOR, clear time, Shadow Rank, and Shadow Shard totals without stretching ornamental corners.
Keep the panel readable against both dark gameplay frames and bright exposure reset flashes by using a deep panel body and gold rim.

context.usage: Shown after floor completion to report rank, time, and shard count before the player chooses next floor or returns to menu.

layout_notes: Reserve a centered 440x72 px title zone for FLOOR CLEAR or FLOOR XX / 30.
Reserve separate 280x56 px zones for clear time, rank letter, and shard count with 32 px vertical spacing.
Keep the lower 220 px band free for two button labels without overlapping the decorative frame.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas canvas is exactly 1920x1080 px for four 960x540 frames with no frame gutter.
Every frame preserves clear title, stat, and button-safe zones with no ornament overlap.
Rank-accent variants remain readable at 75% preview scale and clearly differentiate neutral, S-rank, and A-rank presentations.
